refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable monitoring, controlling, and optimizing energy consumption to meet the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad and high-level. Refining it into sub-FRs focused on monitoring energy use, controlling operational parameters, and optimizing energy efficiency provides a clearer decomposition aligned with measurable and actionable functions.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify by measuring energy_per_ton over the evaluation period and confirming it meets or is below E_target; validate monitoring data accuracy, control responsiveness, and optimization effectiveness.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor energy consumption and tonnage moved in real-time.
FR2: Control vehicle operational parameters to minimize energy use.
FR3: Optimize energy efficiency through adaptive strategies and feedback.

candidate_dps: DP1: Deploy energy and load sensors integrated with telemetry systems.
DP2: Implement vehicle control algorithms for energy-efficient operation.
DP3: Develop adaptive optimization software modules using feedback loops.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1, DP2, and DP3 combined to address monitoring, control, and optimization respectively.

potential_sources: Data dependency between monitoring and control modules.
Feedback loop coupling between optimization and control.
coupling_check.notes: Coupling is expected and manageable with clear interface definitions and modular design.

assumptions_risks: Assumes accurate and reliable sensor data.
Risk of control algorithm complexity impacting real-time responsiveness.
Optimization effectiveness depends on quality of feedback data.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider modularizing optimization to allow iterative improvements without impacting core control.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical components including sensors and embedded control systems responsible for energy monitoring and control.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Sends monitored energy and operational data to NGHE Solution for analysis.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives control commands and optimization parameters from NGHE Solution.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of sensing and control functions; low diffusion as core control logic is embedded.
Contains optimization and data analysis; moderate diffusion due to interaction with Vehicle and Teleoperation.
Limited containment for energy efficiency; mostly external control interface.
Minimal direct involvement in energy efficiency functions.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Energy monitoring and control are best contained within the Vehicle module to minimize interface complexity and latency.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with NGHE Solution optimization logic causing interface complexity.
Risk of embedded control software updates impacting vehicle safety and reliability.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts between Vehicle and NGHE Solution modules; establish rigorous change control for embedded software.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FRs are well decomposed and mapped to logical DPs that address monitoring, control, and optimization comprehensively.

diffusion_risks: Optimization logic diffusion across NGHE Solution and Vehicle modules.
Telemetry interface load and latency impacting real-time control.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support Vehicle as primary owner of monitoring and control; NGHE Solution owns optimization and analysis.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by integrated system testing measuring energy_per_ton and validating interface data flows.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of monitoring and control to Vehicle, optimization to NGHE Solution, with well-defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Refine interface specifications between Vehicle and NGHE Solution to reduce diffusion risks.
Plan for safety impact assessment of embedded control software updates.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition, DP selection, and IM assignment satisfy the requirements and constraints with manageable risks and clear verification paths.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor energy consumption and tonnage moved in real-time.
FR2: Control vehicle operational parameters to minimize energy use.
FR3: Optimize energy efficiency through adaptive strategies and feedback.

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Modular software architecture supports clear interface definitions.
open_risks: Embedded control software updates impacting safety.
evidence_needed: Test data validating energy_per_ton meets E_target.
Interface performance metrics under operational conditions.
